description: Not showing or feeling nervousness, anger, or other strong emotions; still and tranquil. 'Shena' names the settled state—when inner waters are still, when breath flows easy, when the mind rests in present awareness.
sound_symbolism: The word settles into stillness. 'She' /ʃe/ begins hushed, like a finger to lips. Then 'na' grounds with nasal stability, resting in earth.
grammatical_notes: As adjective: 'shena phialu' (calm water). As verb: to calm. Calm itself, the noun, comes by the event-noun rule. Related to 'phaelu' (peaceful) but emphasizes stillness over harmony; 'noalu' (tranquil) names the deeper settled form.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Calm is the foundation of meditation—the settled mind that can observe without reactivity.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Calm communication prevents escalation—speaking from stillness rather than agitation.
